reforms.description: The Polish government facilitated the tax deductibility of R&D spending, allowing firms to benefit from reduced tax liabilities when investing in research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging R&D investment can lead to increased innovation, productivity, and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax deductibility of R&D spending was introduced as part of the anti-crisis plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the fiscal treatment of R&D, encouraging firms to invest more in innovation.
reforms.source_quote: facilitating the tax deductibility of R&D spending.

reforms.description: Poland aims to increase R&D expenditure by enhancing tax credits for research activities, which are currently low compared to the OECD average. This reform is intended to stimulate private sector investment in R&D and improve the overall quality of public research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D tax credits is expected to incentivize private investment in research, leading to higher innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is discussed as a direct way to boost R&D expenditure.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant effort to enhance R&D funding mechanisms, which could lead to improved innovation outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: One direct way to boost R&D expenditure would be to increase tax credits, especially given their currently low levels compared to the OECD average.

reforms.description: The Polish government is working on a comprehensive reform of the higher education system to improve the quality of institutions and align them with market needs. This includes assessing the quality of higher education and simplifying the student-loan scheme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving higher education quality is expected to enhance the skill set of graduates, thereby increasing the human capital available for innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The currently discussed reform of the higher education system could foster FDI absorption and export performance.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to significantly enhance the quality of higher education, which is crucial for developing a skilled workforce that can drive innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The currently discussed reform of the higher education system could foster FDI absorption and export performance by: systematically assessing the quality of higher education institutions.

reforms.description: Poland's innovation system is set to be strengthened by improving the alignment between the demand and supply for skills, enhancing competition among higher education institutions, and increasing support for commercial R&D activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the innovation system, the reform aims to boost productivity and competitiveness, which can lead to higher economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The innovation system should be beefed up by better matching demand and supply for skills.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at enhancing the overall innovation capacity of the economy, which is crucial for long-term growth.
reforms.source_quote: The innovation system should be beefed up by better matching demand and supply for skills, greater competition between higher education institutions and more support for commercial R&D.

reforms.description: The Polish government implemented a tax break of up to EUR 0.1 million per firm on investments for start-ups created in 2008-09, aimed at stimulating business investment during the economic downturn.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages the establishment of new firms, which can enhance innovation and job creation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax break was introduced as part of the anti-crisis plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: The tax break is a significant measure to support new businesses, contributing to the overall economic recovery strategy.
reforms.source_quote: A tax break up to EUR 0.1 million per firm on investments for start-ups created in 2008-09 was implemented.

reforms.description: The Polish government enhanced tax incentives for research activities to stimulate innovation and investment in R&D during the economic crisis.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increases private sector investment in R&D, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Tax incentives for research activities have been enhanced as part of the anti-crisis measures.
reforms.importance_rationale: The enhancement of tax incentives is a significant step to boost R&D investment, which is vital for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Tax incentives for research activities have been enhanced.

reforms.description: Poland is set to receive EUR67.3 billion from the EU's Structural and Cohesion Funds over 2007-2013, with a significant portion allocated to R&D, innovation, and entrepreneurship, highlighting the importance of EU funding for enhancing the country's innovation capacity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The funding is expected to enhance R&D and innovation activities, thereby contributing to economic growth and productivity improvements.
reforms.status_evidence: Poland has become the largest beneficiary of EU cohesion policy with substantial funding allocated.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant influx of funding aimed at bolstering R&D and innovation, which is crucial for Poland's economic development.
reforms.source_quote: Poland has become the largest beneficiary of EU cohesion policy, with EUR67.3 billion allocated over 2007-13.

reforms.description: In February 2009, a new Act on Public-Private Partnerships (PPPs) was enacted to ease the administrative burden discouraging PPP agreements, allowing for the combination of PPP with financing through EU funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates investment in infrastructure and services by reducing barriers for private sector participation, which can enhance efficiency and service delivery.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act on PPPs was enacted to facilitate private sector involvement.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the regulatory framework for PPPs, which can lead to improved infrastructure development and service delivery.
reforms.source_quote: In February 2009, a new Act on PPPs was enacted that eases the administrative burden discouraging PPP agreements.

reforms.description: The Polish government implemented the 'Better Regulation' programme to improve the regulatory environment for businesses, which includes creating one-stop shops for start-ups and enhancing the role of the Polish Agency for Enterprise Development to focus on innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying regulations and improving legal procedures, the programme aims to enhance the business environment, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures taken in 2008 include various initiatives aimed at improving the regulatory environment.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant overhaul of the regulatory framework affecting businesses, which is crucial for enhancing the overall innovation capacity of the economy.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Better Regulation' programme is the most significant reform aimed at improving the regulatory environment for doing business through simplified regulations and more efficient legal procedures.

reforms.description: Poland's innovation policy is outlined in the Strategy for increasing the innovativeness of the economy for 2007-13, which aims to enhance enterprise innovation through various tools, including the Operational Programme 'Innovative Economy' with a substantial EU funding allocation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The allocation of significant EU funds to support R&D and innovation activities is expected to boost domestic research capacity and attract foreign investment in R&D.
reforms.status_evidence: EUR9.7 billion is allocated to this programme, with a significant EU contribution.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing innovation across various sectors, supported by substantial funding, which is critical for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Current Polish innovation policy is laid out in the document Strategy for increasing the innovativeness of the economy for the years 2007-13.

reforms.description: The Polish government has implemented measures to promote lifelong learning, including reimbursement of training costs for employers, aimed at enhancing the skills of low-qualified and older workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the skills of the workforce enhances productivity and adaptability to changing labor market demands.
reforms.status_evidence: Several measures are currently in place to support training for workers.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative addresses a significant gap in workforce skills, which is crucial for economic development and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: Employers can receive reimbursement of up to 50% of the training costs, and the eligibility conditions were eased in 2009.

reforms.description: Poland has established the National Centre for Research and Development to implement R&D and innovation policy, manage strategic R&D programmes, and facilitate technology transfer to the economy.
reforms.component_name: National Centre for Research and Development
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of a dedicated agency enhances coordination and effectiveness of R&D efforts, promoting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The centre was established to manage strategic R&D programmes and facilitate technology transfer.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of the National Centre represents a structural change in Poland's approach to R&D and innovation policy, aiming to enhance collaboration between public research and industry.
reforms.source_quote: The National Centre for Research and Development was established to implement R&D and innovation policy.

reforms.description: The Polish government has initiated the 'Passport to Export' programme, which finances consulting evaluations and promotional events for SMEs looking to enter foreign markets, aiming to enhance their export capabilities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating SMEs' entry into international markets, the programme is expected to enhance their competitiveness and contribute to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is directed at SMEs wanting to start an export activity.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant effort to support SMEs in internationalisation, which is crucial for their growth and the overall economy.
reforms.source_quote: The aim of 'Passport to Export' is to promote a comprehensive model of entering a foreign market with export goods.

reforms.description: The Polish government has developed a range of export promotion programmes, including subsidies for product certificates and trade promotion projects, aimed at enhancing the international competitiveness of Polish SMEs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These programmes are designed to reduce barriers to export for SMEs, thereby increasing their market reach and potential for growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Economy believes strongly that promoting exports can have a long-lasting and sustainable effect on economic growth.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of these programmes indicates a significant policy shift towards supporting SME exports, which is vital for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The first set of instruments is in the form of deminimis public support and includes subsidies for product certificates, publishing and trade promotion projects.

reforms.description: Poland aims to strengthen linkages between industry and universities by facilitating researcher mobility and providing financial incentives for scientific partnerships with firms and international collaboration among research institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between academia and industry is expected to foster innovation and improve the commercialization of research outcomes.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative to strengthen linkages has been put into practice.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance collaboration between research institutions and the private sector, which is crucial for driving innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Strengthen linkages between industry and universities by facilitating the mobility of researchers in and out of business and by providing financial incentives to the development of scientific partnerships with firms.
